reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct. The seenGossipBlockInput cache (SeenBlockInput class in seenGossipBlockInput.ts:85) keys by blockRootHex, which is computed as the hash of the BeaconBlockHeader (gossipHandlers.ts:296). Two equivocating data columns (same slot, proposer, index but different content/signatures) have different blockRootHex values because they reference different block bodies, so both pass validation. Unlike block validation which uses seenBlockProposers keyed by (slot, proposer) to prevent equivocation, data columns lack this per-(slot, proposer, index) deduplication. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker (malicious proposer) can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending two DataColumnSidecars for (slot=N, proposer=V, index=I) with different content and valid signatures, causing both to be accepted and stored under different blockRootHex keys, enabling equivocation attacks. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry PROP-6a4369e9-inv-013 in 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json (lines 298-314) requires 'Anti-equivocation cache must be updated for all imported data columns regardless of publishing' with assertion 'forall col: imported(col) => anti_equivocation_cache.contains(col.key)'. The code does NOT implement per-(slot, proposer, index) anti-equivocation tracking; it only deduplicates by (blockRootHex, index). Severity: Maintained at High per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json criteria (affects >33% of network via trivial gossip-based equivocation attack on PeerDAS-enabled nodes).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Anti-equivocation cache must be updated for all imported data columns regardless of publishing. (PROP-6a4369e9-inv-013, 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json)
